title: Expressions, operators and conversions

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Expressions, operators and conversions; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: C++ expressions combine operators, conversions, value categories, sequencing, overloads, and temporary lifetimes to compute values and effects.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Parentheses make arithmetic grouping explicit while a checked conversion prevents silent narrowing into a smaller destination. Your goal is to trace and repair mixed-type expressions including narrowing and overflow risks. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Program structure and main; pf state expressions; Fundamental types and initialization. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: C++ expressions combine operators, conversions, value categories, sequencing, overloads, and temporary lifetimes to compute values and effects. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Operator overloading can change called behavior but not precedence, signed overflow remains undefined, and dangling views or references can outlive temporaries. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: Overloading an operator changes its precedence and evaluation grouping for the class.

includes: Evaluate precedence, conversions, signedness and numeric boundaries deliberately.
Trace and repair mixed-type expressions including narrowing and overflow risks.
excludes: Operator overloading can change called behavior but not precedence, signed overflow remains undefined, and dangling views or references can outlive temporaries.

outcomes: Trace and repair mixed-type expressions including narrowing and overflow risks.

examples.title: Expressions, operators and conversions: defined case
examples.situation: Parentheses make arithmetic grouping explicit while a checked conversion prevents silent narrowing into a smaller destination.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: Parentheses make arithmetic grouping explicit while a checked conversion prevents silent narrowing into a smaller destination.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.

examples.title: Expressions, operators and conversions: boundary case
examples.situation: ready && evaluate() short-circuits the right operand, but overloaded operators should not be assumed to preserve built-in short-circuit semantics.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: ready && evaluate() short-circuits the right operand, but overloaded operators should not be assumed to preserve built-in short-circuit semantics.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Operator overloading can change called behavior but not precedence, signed overflow remains undefined, and dangling views or references can outlive temporaries.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: Overloading an operator changes its precedence and evaluation grouping for the class.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Trace and repair mixed-type expressions including narrowing and overflow risks.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Overloading an operator changes its precedence and evaluation grouping for the class.
misconceptions.correction: Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: Overloading an operator changes its precedence and evaluation grouping for the class.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Expressions, operators and conversions: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Operator overloading can change called behavior but not precedence, signed overflow remains undefined, and dangling views or references can outlive temporaries.
